disk_measurements.morphology: first sub-mm detection of cold Kuiper-analog ring; bolometer map at 850 micron with ~14 arcsec beam resolved ring-like structure at ~60 AU

warm inner asteroid-belt analog at ~3 AU from Spitzer/IRS 2-component SED decomposition; canonical inner-belt source. Model belt T ~120 K (observed range 100-150 K). Unresolved: inclination 33.7 deg / PA -1.1 deg assumed coplanar with the resolved 70 AU ring (Booth 2023) for rendering.

cold component of Spitzer/IRS+MIPS 2-component SED fit, color temperature ~55 K (audit 2026-05-29: was 35 K, which is the Greaves 1998 SCUBA value); SED-only, geometry refined later by MacGregor 2015 ALMA

narrow cold ring; semi-major-axis center 69.6+/-0.3 AU, FWHM width 10.5+/-0.5 AU (~64-75 AU), inclination 33.7+/-0.5 deg, PA -1.1+/-1.0 deg (=358.9), small eccentricity (e<0.1; star-ring offset <9 AU). Canonical cold-ring geometry.
disk_measurements.notes: Geometry from Booth et al. 2023 (highest-resolution full-disk ALMA fit) -- supersedes the MacGregor 2015 ring-center-only value (64.4 AU was the SMA center, not the inner edge). Inclination 33.7 deg / PA -1.1 deg also adopted (coplanar assumption) for the unresolved inner asteroid + intermediate belts.

disk_measurements.morphology: inner warm component in Booth 2017 three-belt decomposition; SED-derived, geometry from Backman 2009

intermediate dust population at ~20 AU from Herschel SED decomposition (Greaves 2014); re-attributed 2026-05-29 from Booth 2017, which resolves only the 69 AU ring and finds no strong 20 AU emission. Unresolved: inclination 33.7 deg / PA -1.1 deg assumed coplanar with the resolved ring (Booth 2023) for rendering.
Northern arc of cold ring imaged with ALMA; refines geometry + inclination 34+/-2 deg; integrated three-belt dust mass ~0.04 M_earth
inner warm belt in Su 2017 Genie three-belt sculpting model; geometry inherited from Backman 2009, refined by Spitzer/MIPS imaging within 25 AU
intermediate belt in Su 2017 Genie model; required by SED decomposition between asteroid-analog and Kuiper-analog rings; canonical three-belt synthesis source
cold ring in Su 2017 Genie three-belt sculpting model; geometry from MacGregor 2015 ALMA, mass from Booth 2017; planetary sculptors inferred
narrow eccentric ring (e=0.11) with sharp inner edge
disk_measurements.notes: HST/ACS coronagraphy first resolved the main ring; eccentricity e~0.11; sharp inner edge interpreted as gravitational sculpting by an unseen shepherd body

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring, width Delta_a/a~0.13, e=0.11+/-0.01; vertical opening angle 1.0+/-0.25 deg from midplane (exponential scale height)
disk_measurements.notes: ALMA Cycle 0 millimeter imaging; constrains shepherd planet mass to < 3 M_Jup; refines geometry of the main ring. aspect_ratio h=H/r=0.0175 derived from the reported vertical opening angle 1.0+/-0.25 deg from the midplane (h=tan(theta)); the only vertical-structure measurement for this disk (MacGregor 2017 used a 2D model and only cites this).

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring; inclination refined
disk_measurements.notes: ALMA Cycle 4 higher-resolution follow-up; refines inclination to 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg; canonical geometry source for the main cold ring. Eccentricity e=0.11 from Kalas et al. 2005 (HST) / Boley et al. 2012 (ALMA) — the star sits off-centre in the ring. PA 336.28 deg and argument of pericenter 38.04 deg from Gaspar et al. 2023 (apsidally misaligned with the inner disk / intermediate belt at PA ~329.6 deg — the multi-planet-shepherd signature).

disk_measurements.morphology: broad drawn-out inner disk; deprojected outer boundary ~77 AU (periapsis) to ~96 AU (apoapsis), truncated by the inner gap at ~78 AU. Inner limit ~10 AU is the saturation edge. Inclined ~47.8 deg at PA 329.6 deg, strongly misaligned with the ~64.4 deg / PA 336.3 deg outer cold ring (the headline JWST result). Not a narrow asteroid-belt analog; PR-drag-fed continuous emission rather than a discrete warm ring.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI 15.5 and 23 um imaging revises the pre-JWST SED-inferred 8-15 AU / ~150 K narrow warm component (Su 2013) into a broad inner disk. outer_radius_au set to the ~78 AU inner gap (deprojected boundary swings 77 AU periapsis to 96 AU apoapsis) so it no longer overlaps the intermediate belt. Inner-disk fit: inclination 47.8 deg, PA 329.6 deg (Gaspar 2023) -- significantly misaligned with the 64.4 deg outer cold ring (White 2017: 65.6 deg), the headline JWST finding. The earlier 67.52 deg / 336.28 deg recorded here was the outer-ring deprojection orientation, NOT the inner-disk fit. Radial values are profile extents; no formal fit-parameter error bars in the paper.

disk_measurements.morphology: eccentric narrow belt; semi-major-axis edges a~83 AU (e=0.31, inner) to a~104 AU (e=0.265, outer), width 7-20 AU, PA ~329.6 deg (co-oriented with the inner disk), misaligned (apsidal) relative to the outer cold ring. Inner gap at ~78 AU separates it from the broad inner disk; outer gap ~104-134 AU separates it from the main cold ring. Likely shepherded by an unseen planet.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI resolves a previously unknown eccentric intermediate belt. inner/outer_radius_au are the deprojected semi-major-axis edges a~83 and a~104 AU (Gaspar 2023); the earlier ~60-110 AU figure was the eccentric periapsis-apoapsis swing, which read as a false overlap with the inner disk when drawn as a plain annulus. Eccentricity e~0.3 and apsidal misalignment vs. the main cold ring argue for resonant sculpting by an unseen intermediate planet.

disk_measurements.morphology: main cold ring, resolved at 15.5 and 23 um
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI mid-IR imaging of the full multi-belt system; geometry parameters of the main ring not re-derived since ALMA (White 2017) is the canonical mm geometry source. Gaspar 2023 inclination 67.52 deg adopted for deprojection consistent with White 2017's 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg.

disk_measurements.morphology: extrasolar Kuiper-belt analog; cold component SED fit
disk_measurements.notes: Herschel/PACS far-IR imaging of the cold main ring; SED fit yields T_dust ~ 65 K; canonical dust-temperature source for the cold ring

disk_measurements.morphology: mm-cm grain population of the main cold ring
disk_measurements.notes: SONS legacy survey submm photometry; main-ring dust mass ~1.5e-2 M_Earth in mm-cm grains; lower-bound imaged-grain mass (collisional cascade modeling implies 4-10 M_Earth in planetesimal reservoir)

disk_measurements.morphology: IRAS 60 and 100 micron photometric excess; first 'Vega-like' debris disk detection
disk_measurements.notes: Discovery paper; no spatial resolution, geometry fields null

disk_measurements.morphology: first sub-mm resolved image of Vega's cold debris ring
disk_measurements.notes: 850 micron imaging; established cold ring structure but radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: OVRO 1.3 mm interferometric imaging; early sub-mm structure mapping of cold belt
disk_measurements.notes: Wilner, Holman, Kuchner & Ho 2002, 'Millimeter-Wave Aperture Synthesis Imaging of Vega: Evidence for a Ring Arc at 95 AU'; bibcode added 2026-05-29 audit (was null); specific radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: continuous warm inner disk (PR-drag-fed), inner edge ~3-5 AU out to a shallow gap near ~60 AU; NOT a discrete 14 AU asteroid belt (the pre-JWST Su 2013 two-temperature model is superseded). Near pole-on (i~7 deg), T ~130-170 K.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI imaging (Su et al. 2024, arXiv:2410.23636) resolves the inner emission as a smooth continuous disk fed by PR drag, not the discrete ~14 AU warm belt of the Su 2013 SED model. Near pole-on (i~7-11 deg disk, 6.2 deg stellar); PA unconstrained (too face-on). Shallow gap at ~60 AU possibly shepherded by a <6 M_earth planet.

disk_measurements.morphology: broad cold Kuiper-analog outer belt, semi-major-axis edges ~80-170 AU (peak surface brightness ~92 AU), steep inner edge near 80 AU; truly circular (e<=0.002, no star-center offset). T ~50 K.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI (Su et al. 2024) + ALMA (Matra et al. 2020, 2020ApJ...898..146M) resolve a broad circular cold belt ~80-170 AU, revising the Su 2013 SED inner edge (110 AU) and Sibthorpe 2010 (70-200 AU). Eccentricity <=0.002 (measured circular). Near pole-on (i~7-11 deg); PA unconstrained.

disk_measurements.morphology: Herschel-PACS resolved imaging; cold belt extends to ~200 AU with relatively sharp outer edge, sharper inner edge than outer
disk_measurements.notes: 70/100/160 micron imaging; 70 AU inner-edge value (Su 2013 prefers 62 AU); outer-edge 200 AU is canonical for outer_radius_au

disk_measurements.morphology: SMA/early-ALMA 880 micron observations confirm cold-belt structure and constrain inner edge
disk_measurements.notes: Confirmation paper; specific radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: edge-on debris disk, first HST/ACS coronagraphic imaging; inner edge ~17–43 AU (broad), outer extension > 200 AU
edge-on debris disk: resolved planetesimal birth ring ~22-42 AU (peak ~40 AU) plus an extended small-grain halo to ~210 AU. Inclination 88.5 deg (very nearly edge-on), PA 128.48 deg.
disk_measurements.notes: Outer halo extent ~210 AU from HST/STIS (Schneider 2014). Precise inclination 88.5 deg (+0.3/-0.2) and PA 128.50 deg from resolved-mm vertical-structure fits (Daley et al. 2019, 2019ApJ...875...87D; Vizgan et al. 2022, 2022ApJ...935..131V), correcting the earlier 89.5 deg; birth-ring inner edge ~22 AU (Vizgan 2022).

disk_measurements.morphology: five fast-moving south-east-ansa density features, radial outflow 4–10 km/s (faster than Keplerian)
disk_measurements.notes: First detection of moving substructures (SPHERE polarimetric imaging). Inclination quoted to within ~1° of edge-on.

disk_measurements.morphology: multi-epoch confirmation of moving substructures; opacity ratio consistent with mid-range disk visibility
disk_measurements.notes: Two-year SPHERE monitoring; sets velocity precision and the wind-launched (not Keplerian) interpretation. Adopted for cfg disk_opacity = 0.4 tie-break.

disk_measurements.morphology: cold Spitzer IRS SED component (IRAS-discovered disk follow-up)
disk_measurements.notes: Cold-component dust temperature anchor (~50 K).

disk_measurements.morphology: birth-ring planetesimal belt at r_BR ~ 43 AU supplying dust to outer halo via radiation/wind pressure (audit 2026-05-29: was 35 AU; paper states 43 AU)
disk_measurements.notes: Theoretical birth-ring SED model, r_BR = 43 AU. Dust temperature 50 K removed (audit 2026-05-29: not in this paper; 50 K is from Chen 2005).

disk_measurements.morphology: edge-on; resolved vertical structure -- Gaussian scale height H(r)=h*r with constant aspect ratio h, measured between r~23-41 AU (implies H~1.24 AU at the ~40 AU outer edge)
disk_measurements.notes: Vertical aspect-ratio source -- the only NS disk host with a directly-measured h. h=0.031 (+0.005/-0.004, 16th/84th pct), 3 au-resolution ALMA edge-on (i=88.5 deg). inclination_deg left null so Schneider 2014 (89.5) stays the recommended geometry. Implies stirring bodies >~400 km.

disk_measurements.morphology: warm asteroid-belt analog at ~1 AU; olivine-rich crystalline silicate grains (9.7, 11.3, 23 um features); sub-um grain population; no cold (70 um) excess
disk_measurements.notes: Anchor SED fit (Beichman 2005). L_IR/L_star = 2e-4 fractional luminosity; black-body color temperature ~400 K (fit by eye) -> small grains at ~0.5-1 AU; SED-constrained range 0.7-1.2 AU, best-fit r_max <= 1 AU; no 70 um excess (no cold belt). Dust mass 4.6e-9 M_earth = the observed sub-um (0.25 um) emitting-grain population (Beichman 2005: 2.7e19 g); the collisional-cascade total extrapolated to 10 km parent bodies is ~0.9e-3 M_earth (5.4e24 g, ~64x the solar asteroid belt, very uncertain) -- cf. the non-recommended Lisse 2007 entry. The 0.93 +/- 0.16 AU 'fit' and FWHM ~0.3 AU are Smith/Wyatt/Haniff 2009, not Beichman.

disk_measurements.morphology: HD 69830 unresolved by MIDI/VISIR; only a mid-IR interferometric upper limit on the dust size (< 2.4 AU). Does not independently constrain the warm-belt radii.
disk_measurements.notes: Audit 2026-05-29: prior entry mis-attributed the 0.92-1.16 AU SED range (actually Lisse 2007 / Beichman 2005) to this paper with method sed_fit. Smith, Wyatt & Haniff 2009 is interferometric and reports only a 0.05-2.4 AU unresolved size limit -- it does not provide a fitted belt geometry. Demoted from recommended; recommended geometry moved to Beichman 2005.

disk_measurements.morphology: post-collisional debris from a recent (<= 1 Myr) catastrophic disruption of a dwarf-planet-class body; thermally processed sub-um crystalline olivine dominant; planet d's 2:1 and 5:2 outer MMRs at 1.00 and 1.16 AU coincide with the belt and identify d as the dynamical sculptor
disk_measurements.notes: SED-fit grain composition model applied to Beichman 2005 IRS spectrum. Dust mass 1e-3 M_earth is the implied parent-body mass under the catastrophic-event interpretation, not a direct dust mass measurement. Identifies d's 2:1 + 5:2 outer mean-motion resonances at 1.00 / 1.16 AU as the belt's dynamical sculptors.

disk_measurements.morphology: observed L_IR/L_star ~ 2e-4 exceeds steady-state collisional cascade limit by ~10x; requires either recent stochastic event (favoring Lisse 2007 catastrophic interpretation) or an exceptionally massive parent reservoir
disk_measurements.notes: Theoretical steady-state collisional-cascade limit interpretation, not a direct disk measurement. Included as context for the catastrophic-vs-steady-state divergence preserved in the Phase 3 synthesis.

disk_measurements.morphology: single broad cold belt (KBO analog), resolved ~30-150 AU, nearly edge-on (i~77 deg), PA ~65 deg; nearly flat radial surface-density profile.
disk_measurements.notes: Inclination 77+/-4 deg, PA ~65 deg resolved by Herschel (Wyatt 2012); ALMA+SCUBA-2 (Marino et al. 2017, 2017MNRAS.469.3518M) give i=82+/-4, PA=59+/-5 and outer R_max~153 AU (outer radius revised 96 -> 150). Temperature 68 K (Wyatt 2012; the earlier 50 K belonged to HD 38858 in the same paper). Dust mass: SONS 850um gives ~1.6e-3 M_earth (Holland 2017); 0.07 is a KBO-inventory-scaled estimate retained for continuity.

disk_measurements.morphology: broad single cold belt ~6-55 AU (no resolved inner gap), nearly face-on (i~35 deg), PA ~105 deg; width Delta_R/R > 0.75.
disk_measurements.notes: Inclination 35+/-10 deg and PA 105+/-10 deg resolved by Herschel (Lawler et al. 2014, 2014MNRAS.444.2665L) and confirmed by ALMA (MacGregor 2016): inner 6.2 AU, outer 52 AU (ALMA) / 55 AU (Herschel). Dust mass 1.2 M_earth and T~60 K are from Greaves et al. 2004 (2004MNRAS.351L..54G).

disk_measurements.morphology: submm excess detection; KBO analog
two-component SED fit; superseded by MacGregor 2016 resolved imaging
